name: MeeSeva Services
issuingAuthority: Government of Telangana — IT & Electronics Department

sections.purpose: MeeSeva (meaning 'At Your Service') is the Government of Telangana's official citizen service delivery platform. It provides over 600+ government services through 3,500+ MeeSeva centres across Telangana. Services are available both online at tg.meeseva.gov.in and in person at centres. MeeSeva covers: Revenue certificates (Income, Caste, Residence, Birth, Death), Civil Supplies (Ration Card), Registration, Transport, Labour, Municipal services, Social welfare, Agriculture, Finance, and 30+ other departments.
sections.eligibility: Any citizen of Telangana can use MeeSeva services. Services are available to residents, NRIs with Telangana address, and businesses operating in Telangana. No special eligibility for using MeeSeva as a platform — eligibility for specific services depends on the service applied for.
sections.fees: MeeSeva service charges: ₹10–₹50 per service depending on the certificate type. Revenue certificates (Income, Caste, Residence): ₹35–₹45. Birth/Death certificates: ₹25–₹50. Civil Supplies: ₹10–₹35. Some services are free (e.g., Voter ID). Online services may have same or lower fee. Fee is paid at the centre or online.
sections.processingTime: Same-day to 45 days depending on the service. Revenue certificates: 7–30 days. Birth/Death certificates: 3–7 days. Caste certificates: 15–30 days. Online applications have the same processing time as physical. Track status using the Reference Number.
applicationSteps: Step 1 — Visit nearest MeeSeva centre (find at tg.meeseva.gov.in → Locate Centre) or go online at tg.meeseva.gov.in
Step 2 — Select the required department and service
Step 3 — Fill the application form (available at centre or online)
Step 4 — Submit required documents (originals for verification, photocopies for submission)
Step 5 — Pay the service fee (online or at counter)
Step 6 — Collect the Acknowledgment Receipt / Reference Number
Step 7 — Track application status online using Reference Number
Step 8 — Collect certificate when ready (some deliverable via post or SMS)
services: Revenue Department: Income Certificate, Caste Certificate, Residence Certificate, Birth Certificate, Death Certificate, Agricultural Income Certificate, Family Member Certificate
Civil Supplies: Ration Card (new/modification/correction), Fair Price Shop services
Registration: Property registration certificates, Encumbrance Certificate
Transport: Driving Licence related, Vehicle Registration services
Social Welfare: Scholarship applications, Caste/Community certificates
Agriculture: Pahani/Record of Rights, Land records
sections.locateCentre: Find the nearest MeeSeva centre at: tg.meeseva.gov.in → Locate Centre. Select your district, mandal, and village. MeeSeva centres operate in every mandal of Telangana. Timings: typically 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM, Monday to Saturday (closed on government holidays).
commonMistakes: Not carrying original documents — centres require originals for verification even though photocopies are submitted
Visiting on government holidays — centres are closed
Not collecting the Acknowledgement Receipt — needed for status tracking and complaints
Applying at the wrong jurisdiction — apply at the MeeSeva centre in the mandal of your residential address
Using unofficial agents or websites claiming to be MeeSeva
Not having mobile number linked to Aadhaar — delays OTP-based verification

edgeCases.title: MeeSeva application status not updating
edgeCases.problem: Applied at MeeSeva but the application status hasn't changed after the expected processing time.
edgeCases.recommendedNextStep: Call MeeSeva helpline 1800-599-4788 with your Reference Number. Alternatively, visit the MeeSeva centre where you applied. For Revenue certificates, the Tahsildar processes them — visit the Tahsildar office if online status shows 'Pending Verification' for too long.
edgeCases.notes: Processing delays are common during peak periods or if field verification is required. Keep your Reference Number safe throughout.

faqs.question: What is MeeSeva?
faqs.answer: MeeSeva is the Government of Telangana's official citizen service platform with 600+ services across 3,500+ centres. Available online at tg.meeseva.gov.in or at physical centres.

faqs.question: What services are available at MeeSeva?
faqs.answer: MeeSeva offers: Income/Caste/Residence/Birth/Death certificates, Ration Card, Registration, Transport, Labour, Panchayat Raj, Municipal, Social Welfare, Agriculture, Finance services and much more — over 600 services total.

faqs.question: What is the MeeSeva helpline number?
faqs.answer: MeeSeva Helpline: 1800-599-4788 (Toll-Free). Available Monday to Saturday. For online support visit tg.meeseva.gov.in.

faqs.question: How to find nearest MeeSeva centre?
faqs.answer: Visit tg.meeseva.gov.in → Locate Centre. Select your district, mandal, and village. MeeSeva centres are available in every mandal across Telangana.

faqs.question: What are MeeSeva fees?
faqs.answer: MeeSeva service charges: ₹10–₹50 per service. Revenue certificates: ₹35–₹45. Birth/Death certificates: ₹25–₹50. Civil Supplies: ₹10–₹35. Some services are free.

faqs.question: How to track MeeSeva application?
faqs.answer: Track at tg.meeseva.gov.in using your Acknowledgement/Reference Number received at the time of application. You can also call 1800-599-4788 with your Reference Number.

faqs.question: What are MeeSeva working hours?
faqs.answer: MeeSeva centres are typically open 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM, Monday to Saturday. Closed on government holidays. Online services at tg.meeseva.gov.in are available 24/7.
